Written Questions

PARLIAMENTARY ACTIVITIES

Perran's spoken and written contributions working for you in Westminster

Recent Contributions

Here are my recent Parliamentary contributions.

See Older Contributions

Written Question: Department for Business and Trade - Pay (20/10/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to help support people who were underpaid wages by their former employers.

See More

Education: Children with SEND - Mainstream Schools (20/10/2025)

A statement regarding the adversarial nature of the SEND system for all stakeholders and a move to focus on inclusion as a key measure of success.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE

See More

ENGLISH DEVOLUTION AND COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT BILL COMMITTEE (Day 2 - 16/10/2025)

Please click here for a collection of statements and questions made by Perran Moon on Day 2 of the English Devolution Bill Committee.

See More

Written Question: Department for Business and Trade - Funerals: Small Businesses (15/10/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what support her Department is providing small independent funeral directors in response to market pressures from large direct cremation providers?

See More

ENGLISH DEVOLUTION AND COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT BILL COMMITTEE (Day 1 - 14/10/2025)

Please click here for a collection of statements and questions made by Perran Moon on Day 1 of the English Devolution Bill Committee.

See More

Bovine Tuberculosis Control and Badger Culling (13/10/2025)

A speech in a Westminster Hall debate following on from a petition called "End the Badger cull and adopt other approaches to bovine TB control". The petition received 389 signatures from Camborne, Redruth and Hayle.

See More

Written Question: Treasury - Financial Ombudsman Service: Standards (13/10/2025)

To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she is taking to tackle the backlog of cases at the Financial Ombudsman Service.

See More

Written Question: Treasury - Life Insurance (13/10/2025)

To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what regulatory safeguards are in place to protect consumers from excessive premium increases following the transfer of life insurance policy liabilities.

See More

Written Question: Treasury - Life Insurance (13/10/2025)

To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps her Department is taking to regulate the pricing of legacy life insurance policies following provider transfers.

See More

Housing, Communities and Local Government Questions (13/10/2025)

A question asking for reassurances that unleashing the Cornish Celtic Tiger and maximising our economic potential is not reliant on Cornwall joining a Mayoral Combined Authority.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE.

See More

Written Question: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs - Cats and Dogs: Smuggling (08/10/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what will be in the terms of the Carr Hill review.

See More

Written Question: Department for Education - Children: Community Development (23/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to improve community (a) understanding and (b) acceptance of (i) residential and (ii) support services for vulnerable children (A) in the community and (B) in the criminal justice system.

See More

Written Question: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology - Animal Experiments: Regulation (19/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of banning animal testing.

See More

Written Question: Department of Health and Social Care - General Practitioners: Finance (19/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what will be in the terms of the Carr Hill review.

See More

Written Question: Department for Education - Children: Social Services (18/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will launch a public awareness campaign to support the integration of children's social care services into local communities.

See More

Written Question: Department for Work and Pensions - Employment: Chronic Illnesses and Sick Pay (17/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether she has had discussions with employers on (a) the adequacy of employer sick ay top-up schemes and (b) their role in supporting workers with long-term health conditions.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Justice - Prisoners: Mental Health (17/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what recent assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of continued IPP incarceration on the mental health of prisoners; and what steps he is taking to ensure appropriate psychological support is available to those affected.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Local Government: Housing and Planning (17/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he plans to establish a national fund to support planning and housing teams in councils with limited resources or expertise.

See More

Written Question: Department for Education - Young People: Cornwall (11/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent estimate her Department has made of the number and proportion of young people aged 16 to 24 who are not in education, employment or training in (a) Cornwall and (b) Camborne and Redruth constituency.

See More

Regional Transport Inequality (11/09/2025)

A speech on the specific travel needs caused by the the disparity in the effectiveness and adequacy of transport needs due to the unique circumstances of remote coastal areas.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE.

See More

Transport: Electric Vehicles (11/09/2025)

A question asking for a meeting with Ministers to discuss the growing sales of EVs to meet the Government's proactive commitments to reducing transport emissions.

See More

Russian Drones: Violation of Polish airspace (10/09/2025)

A question backing the solidarity of the NATO Alliance and the importance of Article 5 following the violation of Polish Airspace.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE.

Written Question: Department for Health and Social Care - General Practitioners (10/09/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department plans to take to sustain the independent contractor status of GP partnerships through the 10 Year Health Plan.

See More

Adjournment debate: Remote Coastal Communities (08/09/2025)

Perran hosted an Adjournment Debate discussing the effects and particular requirements that need to be considered when ensuring growth and prosperity within remote coastal communities.
CLICK HERE TO READ MORE.

See More

Work and Pensions: Topical Questions (01/09/2025)

A question discussing young NEET people being a reflection of a range of different factors including poverty, poor mental health services, lack of access to support services, inadequate transport, SEND and care services, especially in remote coastal areas such as Camborne, Redruth and Hayle.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE.

See More

Written Question: Department for Education - Pre-school Education: Digital Technology (01/08/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what plans her Department has to help (a) raise the profile and (b) increase levels of use of the Best Start digital service.

See More

Written Question: Department for Education - Pre-School Education: Finance (31/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, with reference to the policy paper entitled 'Giving' every child the best start in life, published on 7th July 2025, what the terms of the review into fairer funding for early years will be.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Justice - Legal Aid Scheme: Housing (28/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what support is available to homeowners who cannot afford legal representation in disputes with housing (a) developers and (b) associations over structural defects.

See More

Written Question: Department of Health and Social Care - Mental Health Services: Cornwall (24/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve (a) child and (b) adult mental health services in Cornwall.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Freehold: Repairs and Maintenance (24/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department plans to expand the (a) remit and (b) powers of the New Homes Ombudsman to include freehold homeowners with unresolved structural issues.

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government: Housing Associations - Standards (24/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that housing associations are held accountable for the (a) quality and (b) safety of homes they (i) manage and (ii) build.

A speech in a debate in the honour of Sir David Amess, highlighting Cornwall's immense strategic and economic value.

Written Question: Ministry of Justice - Discrimination: Women (24/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps is her Department taking to tackle misogyny in (a) the bankruptcy and (b) judicial system.

Written Question: Home Office - Refugees: Ukraine (22/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate her Department has made of the number of people on the (a) Homes for Ukraine and (b) Ukraine Family Schemes who are working in the agricultural sector.

Health and Social Care - Health Inequalities (22/07/2025)

A question covering the work of Carn to Coast GP Surgeries across the constituency, including where my father practised for over 30 years, and the role the Carr-Hill formula plays in the Government's 10 Year Health Plan.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE

Business of the House (17/07/2025)

A question highlighting the importance of Cornish as one of the five Celtic languages, and how it is the only one without Part III Language Status - an issue that must be rectified.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE

See More

Business and Trade - Industrial Strategy: Cornwall (17/07/2025)

A question about Cornwall being uniquely positioned to become a key driver of UK economic growth through its vast renewable energy and critical mineral resources, and how can Cornwall deliver on its potential outside of a Mayoral Combined Authority.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE

See More

Wales: Offshore Wind (16/07/2025)

A question covering the potential of floating offshore wind in the Celtic Sea, which sits between Cornwall and Wales, in terms of not only its potential for the push to net zero but also in terms of the creation of thousands of jobs across south Wales and Cornwall.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE

See More

Energy Security and Net Zero: Topical Questions (15/07/2025)

A question highlighting the potential of geothermal energy in our nation's push to net-zero, as well as the immense contribution that the Cornish granite batholith can make to that market.
CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Shared Ownership Schemes (14/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of resolving shared ownership arrangements of properties where offshore freehold ownership precludes staircasing.

Written Question: Ministry of housing, Communities and Local Government - Shared Ownership Schemes: Reform (14/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to reform the Shared Ownership system.

Written Question: Ministry of housing, Communities and Local Government - Shared Ownership Schemes: Reform (14/07/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment her Department has made of the potential implications for her policies of the recommendations set out in the Fifth Report of the Levelling Up, Housing and Communities Committee on Shared Ownership, Session 2023-24, published on 26 March 2024, HC 61.

Housing, Communities and Local Government: Topical Questions (14/07/2025)

A question highlighting Cornwall's need to access the highest level of devolution, without joining a Mayoral Combined Authority, due to it violating Cornwall's National Minority Status.

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Local Government Finance (30/04/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department plans to take into account low wages in rural areas when determining funding settlements for deprived areas.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Local Government Finance (30/04/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department plans to take into account (a) remoteness and (b) peripherality of areas such as Cornwall when determining funding settlements for deprived areas.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Local Government Finance (30/04/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department plans to take into account super-ageing demographics such as in Cornwall when determining funding settlements for deprived areas.

See More

Written Question: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government - Local Government Finance (30/04/2025)

To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department plans to take into account non-resident population seasonal increases due to tourism when determining funding settlements for deprived areas.

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what plans her Department has to increase funding through the Dedicated Schools Grant to areas with (a) below average allocations and (b) deficits.
